purpose: Claim ledger for the Fable 5 pipeline mission (docs/_handoff/2026-06-11-fable5-mission-prompt.md). Doctrine: optimistic intake (ASSUMED) -> verification (VERIFIED / PARTIAL / REFUTED) -> deepen (gsr_application). Nothing ASSUMED or REFUTED may enter the build plan. This file is the research loop's heartbeat; commit it every cycle.

status_legend.ASSUMED: heard from a source, not yet checked
status_legend.VERIFIED: confirmed against official docs/changelog/live test (evidence cited)
status_legend.PARTIAL: works with caveats (caveat stated in verifier_evidence)
status_legend.REFUTED: checked and false/stale; never build on it

claims.claim: This environment runs Claude Code 2.1.173 with skills: deep-research, loop, verify, run, code-review, security-review, simplify, update-config, session-start-hook, fewer-permission-prompts, claude-api; /goal is NOT exposed in the web harness skill registry.
claims.source: live session audit (claude --version + skill registry)
claims.verifier_evidence: Phase E live test, see 2026-06-11-mission-run-notes.md Addendum: the /loop skill's scheduling backend (CronCreate/ScheduleWakeup) is not exposed here either; 30-min cadence emulated with a persistent Monitor tick. /lanes and /resume-lane DID surface as invocable skills mid-session (run-notes Phase E table corrected).
claims.gsr_application: Goal conditions are emulated as AND-checklists + independent verifier agent; /loop drives checkpoint cadence

claims.claim: YouTube blocks transcript/player API requests from cloud-provider IPs; rung 1 (youtube-transcript-api) fails IpBlocked from this container even though youtube.com returns 200.
claims.source: mission doc + live test video kOKavHnlPik
claims.verifier_evidence: Live test 2026-06-11: youtube_transcript_api raised IpBlocked
claims.gsr_application: Seed-corpus transcript mining must run on Daniel's Mac (rung 2 kit pre-staged in 2026-06-11-transcript-pull-kit/)

claims.claim: Invidious instances can substitute for direct YouTube caption access from a cloud IP (rung 4).
claims.source: mission doc rung 4
claims.verifier_evidence: Live test 2026-06-11: inv.nadeko.net lists caption tracks but serves 0-byte caption bodies; /videos endpoint disabled; yewtu.be 403; pipedapi.kavin.rocks timeout. Metadata sometimes available, transcript content not. Matches 2026 reports that YouTube's PO-token requirement broke caption proxying.
claims.gsr_application: Do not plan rung 4 as a backbone; opportunistic only, as the mission doc already says

claims.claim: /goal needs Claude Code >= v2.1.139; evaluator checks the condition every turn; /goal show + /goal clear exist.
claims.source: mission doc Phase E table (cited code.claude.com/docs/en/goal, verified 2026-06-11 by the doc's author)
claims.verifier_evidence: Doc-verified 2026-06-11 per mission contract; env version 2.1.173 satisfies the floor; feature simply not surfaced in the web harness (CL-001); [video jUL-0O4cu2c] Confirms /goal show displays turns taken, tokens consumed, and goal name. Confirms /goal clear. Confirms 'goal achieved' message on completion.
claims.gsr_application: Daniel's Mac CLI sessions CAN use /goal for build lanes; web sessions emulate

claims.claim: Sample-transcript style hype ('auto mode never does anything crazy', 'the model is smarter than you, lean on it for all thinking') is reliable engineering guidance.
claims.source: video corpus sample transcript (mission doc R1)
claims.verifier_evidence: Contradicts canon s15 prompt-hygiene ruling and Anthropic's own confirmation-gate guidance; the mission doc itself flags it as hype. Logged per doctrine so it is on the record as not-build-on-able.
claims.gsr_application: None. Confirmation gates stay.

claims.claim: Rundown Creator returns errors as HTTP 200 with a JSON error body, so every client must read the body.
claims.source: repo canon (CLAUDE.md, mission doc)
claims.verifier_evidence: Long-established in repo across /api/rc-* implementations; re-confirmed in CLAUDE.md current state
claims.gsr_application: Any new RC automation (Lane 4 sync) wraps calls in a body-checking client

claims.claim: The live DB already has production_lower_thirds / lower_thirds_variations (PR #50's migration applied) while main's deployed code still reads 'graphics' — the rename ships code-side only when PR #47 + #50 + #52 merge.
claims.source: PR #50 body ('Migration listed in Supabase list_migrations (verified applied)') + open-PR audit 2026-06-11
claims.verifier_evidence: Read-only Supabase SQL 2026-06-11: production_lower_thirds, lower_thirds_variations, production_graphics all EXIST (0 rows each); 'graphics'/'graphics_variations' are GONE; episodes=48, guests=175.
claims.gsr_application: First build slice (answer 1A) must sequence on this: DB is ahead of deployed code

claims.claim: cleanupPeriodDays is honored when set in a project's .claude/settings.json (not only user settings).
claims.source: own assumption while wiring Phase E
claims.verifier_evidence: Claude Code settings schema (update-config skill, 2026-06-11): cleanupPeriodDays is a general settings.json key; settings load user -> project -> local and merge. Caveat: THIS session's permission layer blocks settings.json edits (self-modification guard), so applying it falls to Daniel or a Mac session (one-liner recorded in run-notes).
claims.gsr_application: Mission Control needs Daniel's Mac session JSONL to survive; if user-only, the plan tells his Mac to set it once

claims.claim: Production is in a schema-skew window: the live DB no longer has the 'graphics' table (renamed by PR #50's applied migration) while the deployed main-branch app still queries 'graphics', so the deployed lower-thirds pages/API hit a missing table until PR #47+#50+#52 merge and deploy.
claims.source: deduction from live-DB check + open-PR audit
claims.verifier_evidence: Live DB table list 2026-06-11 (graphics absent) + main's code references graphics (PR #50 diff renames ~20 call sites, unmerged). Impact bounded: all lower-thirds tables have 0 rows, so no data loss, but the deployed pages error against a missing relation.
claims.gsr_application: Raises urgency of Daniel's Q16 merge decision: merging the PR stack closes the skew window. Build plan sequences slice 1 after that merge.

claims.claim: Official Agent View (claude agents): all BACKGROUND sessions on one screen, Haiku row summaries, peek/reply, scriptable --json. Gaps: interactive terminals invisible until backgrounded (/bg or left-arrow); NO token columns; no history search.

claims.verifier_evidence: Research preview since v2.1.139 (~2026-05-13); disableAgentView kill switch exists; [video tJVUAzLZUyI] Confirms Agent View (claude agents) shows background sessions; adds specific CLI: 'claude --bg <prompt>' creates background session, Ctrl+X stops, Shift+Tab cycles permission modes, arrow navigation.; [video msiaY6wec90] Confirms Agent View dashboard shows colored status indicators per session. Adds detail: yellow = needs input, green = done. Also confirms 'claude agents' as the CLI command to list all background sessions.; [video ZAaxx3qyT8g] Confirms Agent View shows all sessions, status indicators (yellow=needs input, green=done). Adds left/right arrow navigation, /bg command, Ctrl+X twice to kill, reply-from-view capability.

claims.verifier_evidence: Official docs label Broadcast the recommended method; needs RLS policy on realtime.messages + trigger setup
claims.gsr_application: Live status on /workflow, /lower-thirds, /episodes without refresh; build realtime features broadcast-first

claims.claim: Supabase Cron (pg_cron module) + Supabase Queues (pgmq, GA) are the official scheduled-job + pull-queue pair; pgmq_public exposable via Data API so the Mac worker polls read() with visibility timeout (durability + retries for free).
claims.source: R5 / supabase.com/docs/guides/queues/api + /docs/guides/cron
claims.verifier_evidence: Queues is pull-only (Mac still polls); pgmq_public exposure must be explicitly enabled + RLS-considered
claims.gsr_application: Supersedes the hand-rolled jobs-table plan in canon s12 orchestration (refinement, same shape); distribution backbone

claims.claim: Vercel deploy hooks: per-project unauthenticated URLs (URL = the secret), auto-cancel duplicates, 5/project Pro; observe via REST API with account token (Bearer + ?teamId).
claims.source: R5 / vercel.com/docs/deploy-hooks
claims.verifier_evidence: Treat hook URL as a password; personal tokens team-scoped, set expiration
claims.gsr_application: Automation can redeploy the dashboard and watch the deploy land before reporting success

claims.claim: Basecamp 4 API: OAuth tokens expire after 2 WEEKS (refresh via launchpad); per-project webhooks exist (18+ recording types, 10 retries backoff); rate ~50 req/10s/IP; flat routes canonical; no chat events.
claims.source: R5 / github.com/basecamp/bc3-api
claims.verifier_evidence: Webhook cap per account (507 when exceeded)
claims.gsr_application: Basecamp lane MUST build the token-refresh loop before any webhook consumer ships; push-style sync viable

claims.claim: ProPresenter official HTTP API (openapi.propresenter.com): trigger presentations/cues/props/looks/macros/timers/messages/clear-layer; chunked-HTTP status streaming; TCP wrapper. NO server-side dry-run mode. Version era is now ProPresenter 17+ subscription (not 7.x); Companion module v1.1.1 2026-06-04 active.
claims.source: R5 / support.renewedvision.com + openapi.propresenter.com
claims.verifier_evidence: Safety is procedural: read-only GET verification, then triggers on TEST machine only; pin + record test-machine version before trigger work
claims.gsr_application: Defines the authorized ProPresenter lane: verify-by-GET, fire-on-test, live rig human-yes per canon s15

claims.claim: YouTube Data API quota CHANGED: videos.insert costs 1 unit in a dedicated Video Uploads bucket, default 100 uploads/day (old era ~6/day equivalent). publishAt still requires privacyStatus=private. Unverified API projects: uploads locked private until compliance audit passes.
claims.source: R5 / developers.google.com/youtube/v3/determine_quota_cost
claims.verifier_evidence: Third-party sources date the change Dec 2025; the practical GSR gate is the ONE-TIME project audit
claims.gsr_application: Distribution unblocked quota-wise; publishAt fits the Monday-4PM webstream model; START THE AUDIT EARLY

claims.claim: n8n vs Claude Code 2026: complementary not substitutional; for a solo non-developer on Claude Code + cloud APIs, self-hosted n8n adds ops surface without capability; Cron/Queues + gated routes serve the guardrail role.
claims.source: R5 / blog.n8n.io (Apr 2026)
claims.verifier_evidence: Vendor-side evidence; revisit only if many always-on event integrations outgrow Cron/Queues/webhooks; [video ZeJXI2MAhj0] Confirms n8n and Claude Code are complementary: n8n for deterministic scheduled automations with existing infrastructure; Claude Code for conversational/iterative agentic workflows. Adds Trigger.dev as a third option for deployed agentic workflows.; [video Dug97la9YoQ] Confirms use-case split: n8n for scheduled, data-driven, high-volume, compliance-sensitive automations; Claude Code for iterative/conversational workflows, custom apps, and anything requiring human-in-the-loop back-and-forth.; [video Vc3I-8eQ7rA] Confirms complementary framing. Adds pattern: use Claude Code agentic workflow to prototype and explore, then rebuild the validated solution in n8n for production scheduling.; [video ZeJXI2MAhj0] Confirms n8n knowledge is complementary, not wasted — agentic workflows (Claude Code + trigger.dev) are additive to n8n skills, not a replacement.; [video uUEa6K-FLB8] Confirmed: n8n and Claude Code used together in production — n8n handles workflow orchestration and error detection; Claude Code (via MCP server + skills) handles diagnosis and repair. Video demonstrates a live working integration.; [video tDGiWn0flK8] Confirmed: video explicitly states traditional n8n automation remains ideal for deterministic/repetitive tasks while agentic Claude Code handles variable judgment-call tasks; the two are described as complementary, not competitive.; [video wb4uDdsFh6o] Author explicitly states n8n is not dead and will not be replaced by Claude Code; they are fundamentally different tools and complementary. Also confirms from OCO3aq3G0mk (n8n MCP pattern). Provides the 10-minute rule as a concrete decision heuristic.
claims.gsr_application: ADR-0012's n8n deferral HOLDS; seed-corpus n8n videos are technique sources only

claims.claim: /goal real since v2.1.139; evaluator = prompt-based Stop hook on small fast model every turn; /goal clear exists BUT '/goal show' does NOT (bare /goal prints status). 4000-char conditions; works in -p; survives --resume.
claims.source: R2 / code.claude.com/docs/en/goal
claims.verifier_evidence: Mission doc's '/goal show' is wrong - correction recorded in run-notes; [video jZgcWCzxh1I] Confirmed: /goal described as a depth-first loop checking 'done == true' repeatedly; contrasted with workflows (width-first parallel fan-out). Video clarifies /goal can run 24+ hours if criteria are vague.; [video jUL-0O4cu2c] Confirms /goal keeps working across turns until condition met, consistent with evaluator pattern described in CL-025.
claims.gsr_application: Mac lanes: /goal <AND-list> with a turn-cap clause as cost brake

claims.claim: /loop: s/m/h/d, dynamic 1m-1h; recurring tasks expire 7 days after creation; session-scoped, max 50/session; .claude/loop.md replaces only the bare-/loop maintenance prompt; restored on --resume if unexpired.
claims.source: R2 / code.claude.com/docs/en/scheduled-tasks
claims.verifier_evidence: Loops die with the session; [video fOpFgjH8IfQ] Confirms /loop is built-in; adds that /loop sessions expire after 3 days requiring renewal workaround, and that tmux is necessary for persistence across disconnects.; [video xJ5oz63mIec] Confirms /loop creates session-scoped crons. Clarifies: in terminal /clear preserves crons; in desktop app /clear kills crons. 7-day expiry confirmed in terminal; desktop app shows 3 days (possible UI bug or version difference).; [video jqoFP9QapXI] Confirms /loop for recurring tasks. Video states loops last 3 days (desktop app) vs 7 days (terminal/documentation). Adds: can set loops to auto-kill after N iterations.; [video jqoFP9QapXI] Video states /loop lasts "3 days" — CONFLICTS with CL-026 which says 7 days. This discrepancy is worth flagging; the 7-day figure may reflect a later update to the feature after this video was recorded.; [video OUyfxhFtGCo] Confirms /loop 3-day expiry and recurring task behavior; adds that default interval is 10 min if unspecified and that loops are strictly per-session (cron_list only sees loops from the current session); [video RLjaUES9P8A] /loop confirmed: 'run it without a prompt and Claude will go into maintenance mode and just keep your project tidy' -- handles unfinished tasks, PR comments, merge conflicts on a schedule.
claims.gsr_application: In-day babysitting only; standing schedules go to Routines or Supabase Cron

claims.claim: Routines (research preview): Anthropic cloud, fresh GitHub clone, NO permission prompts, push only to claude/-prefixed branches; triggers scheduled (CLI /schedule) / API /fire / GitHub events (web-only); caps Pro 5 / Max 15 / Team 25 runs/day, min interval 1h.
claims.source: R2 / code.claude.com/docs/en/routines
claims.verifier_evidence: Structurally cannot touch QNAP/ProPresenter (clone + connectors only); [video xn6Z5PYyAIE] Routines explicitly defined in transcript as: 'In Claude, we're able to set up these things called routines. And what routines are is it basically just on a set cadence injects a prompt into Claude code.' The video demonstrates scheduling a routine that references a Google Sheet for content pipeline generation, confirming the inject-on-cadence mechanism described in CL-027.; [video ehg4fhydTgs] Confirms routines run on Anthropic cloud, fresh GitHub clone, no permission carryover. Adds: secrets must go in environment variables (not .env which is gitignored), environment variables set per cloud environment object in settings.; [video xJ5oz63mIec] Confirms routines run on Anthropic cloud with no local file access. Adds three trigger types: schedule, API webhook, GitHub event. Confirms minimum 1hr interval.; [video ctu_RyQNR5w] Confirms Routines run on Anthropic cloud; adds specific run caps (Pro=5/day, Max=15/day, Team/Enterprise=25/day) and hard minimum schedule interval of 1 hour.; [video D3pxbkPg0KE] Confirms remote Routines; adds that Cowork local scheduled tasks skip (then catch up) if app is closed, and that model can be selected per task.; [video fOpFgjH8IfQ] Confirms Routines cloud path; this video uses /loop as the local equivalent and highlights the 3-day session expiry as a key limitation not present in cloud Routines.; [video 6MC1XqZSltw] Remote routines confirmed: fresh GitHub clone in cloud, no permission carryover. Extends with: cloud environment is destroyed after run; files must be pushed back to repo; 'allow unrestricted branch pushes' permission required; API keys must be in environment variables not .env.
claims.gsr_application: Nightly data audits, RC dry-run reports, lanes refresh: the laptop-closed layer (14A)

claims.claim: Agent teams (CLAUDE_CODE_EXPERIMENTAL_AGENT_TEAMS): still experimental + disabled by default as of 2026-06-11; no /resume of teammates, one team at a time, much higher token cost.
claims.source: R2 / code.claude.com/docs/en/agent-teams
claims.verifier_evidence: Requires v2.1.32+; [video vfWTyEreOEc] Agent teams confirmed experimental, must enable in .claude/settings.json. Video confirms they share task list and can talk to each other, distinguishing them from subagents.; [video vDVSGVpB2vc] Confirms agent teams are disabled by default and enabled via CLAUDE_CODE_EXPERIMENTAL_AGENT_TEAMS env var in .claude/settings.local.json (project-level). Video shows the exact setup flow.; [video HlqAe1LL7U8] Second independent confirmation of agent teams setup via settings.json env var; also shows display_mode option (in-process vs split panes) as a setup question Claude asks during configuration.; [video oC3F2SFaF9w] Confirms agent teams is experimental and not enabled by default; requires adding configuration to settings.json. Also confirms that team agents can request information from each other (unlike regular sub-agents which only report back to the orchestrator).; [video jZgcWCzxh1I] Confirmed: agent teams described as 'group chat' where agents share a task list and talk to each other; distinguished from sub-agents (parallel but no inter-agent communication) and workflows (JavaScript-orchestrated fan-out).; [video zm-BBZIAJ0c] Confirms CLAUDE_CODE_EXPERIMENTAL_AGENT_TEAMS is still experimental and disabled by default. Adds: version must be >= 2.1.32, env var belongs in the settings.json 'env' section.; [video y9IYtWELMHw] Confirms experimental flag in settings.json; confirms teammates get CLAUDE.md, MCPs, skills but not lead conversation history.; [video BT_yyd_djGw] Confirms experimental agent teams feature and tmux split-pane mode.; [video StCB3P71CUw] Confirms experimental flag via settings.json env block; confirms tmux or iTerm2 required for split pane.; [video MbKUeufUZIY] Confirms CLAUDE_CODE_EXPERIMENTAL_AGENT_TEAMS=1 in settings.json; confirms multi-session parallel execution.; [video vDVSGVpB2vc] Confirms CLAUDE_CODE_EXPERIMENTAL_AGENT_TEAMS is the env var (placed in settings.local.json not settings.json); adds that teammates inherit permissions and can access all project MCP servers and skills; [video oC3F2SFaF9w] Confirms agent teams require experimental settings.json flag; video adds that tmax is recommended for multi-pane visibility and that Pro plan usage per task can reach ~$7-8 (50% of a Pro session).
claims.gsr_application: Stay on subagents/background sessions; at most a one-off adversarial review

claims.claim: Hooks now span ~30 events and FIVE handler types: command, http, mcp_tool, prompt, agent (experimental). SessionStart/Setup: command+mcp_tool only.
claims.source: R2 / code.claude.com/docs/en/hooks
claims.verifier_evidence: Tutorial at /hooks-guide; [video BlNJFa3Btm8] Confirms hook support for scheduled tasks -- video demonstrates adding a hook so that every time a scheduled task session finishes a sound notification plays, invoked via natural language to Claude; [video G9OBGn0h6LE] Hooks lifecycle confirmed: PreToolUse, PostToolUse, Stop (post-response) trigger points all demonstrated. Stop hook fires when Claude concludes its response. Settings.json hook entry matches on tool name array.; [video RLjaUES9P8A] Confirms ~30 hook events for Claude Code; provides contrast with Codex (~6 events), confirming the 30-event figure and illustrating practical significance.
claims.gsr_application: Mechanical rule enforcement: PreToolUse command hook hard-blocking Tailscale-IP/QNAP-write commands; prompt Stop hook as automated David-Rule check

claims.claim: Agent SDK: list_sessions()/listSessions() real and returns auto-generated per-session summary (SDKSessionInfo.summary); transcript reading is get_session_messages() NOT 'Session.History'; official cookbook 'Building a session browser' exists.
claims.source: R2 / platform.claude.com/cookbook/claude-agent-sdk-05-building-a-session-browser
claims.verifier_evidence: Mission doc's 'Session.History' is wrong - correction recorded; [video TqC1qOfiVcQ] Agent SDK confirmed as Claude Code packaged as a library. Anthropic presenter (Thariq Shihipar) confirms list_sessions / agent loop primitives and sub-agent patterns. File system + bash are the primary tools.; [video xJ5oz63mIec] Confirms Agent SDK stateless by default. Adds: pass session_id to make stateful. SDK auto-compacts but does not /clear. Monthly subscription credit applies to Agent SDK as a separate dedicated budget.
claims.gsr_application: Mission Control extend-lanes build: ~20-line script list_sessions() -> lanes.json sessions block -> lanes.html

claims.claim: Session storage: ~/.claude/projects/<encoded-cwd>/<session-id>.jsonl, 30-day default cleanup, --continue/--resume <name|id>, --from-pr <number>, named sessions (claude -n, /rename), /branch + --fork-session.

claims.claim: /effort: low/medium/high/xhigh/max on Fable 5 + Opus 4.8/4.7; default high (xhigh on 4.7); ultracode = setting (xhigh + dynamic workflows), session-only; 'ultrathink' the only special keyword now.

claims.verifier_evidence: ; [video jZgcWCzxh1I] Confirmed: /effort levels low/medium/high/xhigh/max shown in use; video introduces a 6th level 'ultra code' above max that auto-invokes dynamic workflows on every prompt.; [video q5lg3npxjAc] Confirms effort levels low/medium/high/xhigh/max. Adds 'ultra code' as a 6th tier (xhigh+workflows). Confirms default is high.

claims.claim: Checkpointing: /rewind or Esc Esc (only with empty prompt); bash-modified files NOT tracked, nor external edits/other sessions; rewind menu has Summarize from/up-to here.
claims.source: R2 / code.claude.com/docs/en/checkpointing
claims.verifier_evidence: ; [video l4mSSN6exGg] Confirms /rewind usage; Anthropic's own non-engineering teams use checkpoint-then-restart rather than correction as their primary recovery pattern.; [video _qZvORxGqI0] Confirms /rewind and double-Esc for checkpointing. Adds: /rewind menu includes a 'summarize from here' option that generates a handoff summary for the next session.; [video jqoFP9QapXI] Confirms /rewind rolls back conversation to a checkpoint. Also confirms bash-modified files tracked.
claims.gsr_application: Git discipline + Type-YES gates stay the safety net for scripted ops

claims.claim: Changelog May 1 - Jun 11: /goal (2.1.139), Agent View (2.1.139-145), auto mode expanded (2.1.143-152), background sessions in /resume + pinned stay alive (2.1.144/147), dynamic workflows + Opus 4.8 xhigh (2.1.154), Stop/SubagentStop hooks return additionalContext (2.1.163), fallbackModel chains of 3 (2.1.166), post-session hook + /cd + --safe-mode (2.1.169), Fable 5 (2.1.170), nested subagents 5 deep (2.1.172).
claims.source: R2 / github.com/anthropics/claude-code CHANGELOG + whats-new
claims.verifier_evidence: Version-date mapping via code.claude.com/docs/en/whats-new

Reviewer notes token drain is invisible — reinforces that background agents provide zero cost visibility in the dashboard.; [video ZAaxx3qyT8g] Confirms Agent View is CLI-only and shows session runtime. claims.gsr_application: Pair Agent View with ccusage as the cost lens (CL-013)

claims.claim: ergut/youtube-transcript-mcp (remote, Cloudflare Workers): live and responding 2026-06-11; transcript fetch executes on Cloudflare's edge IP, not the session's; 7-day KV cache; no keys.
claims.source: R3 / github.com/ergut/youtube-transcript-mcp (endpoint live-checked)
claims.verifier_evidence: claude mcp add --transport sse youtube-transcript https://youtube-transcript-mcp.ergut.workers.dev/sse; thin maintenance (1-person)
claims.gsr_application: The ONLY vetted path that may unblock transcript mining from CLOUD sessions; INSTALL candidate (one-tap yes, then live-test whether YouTube serves Cloudflare)

claims.claim: jkawamoto/mcp-youtube-transcript (local, uvx, yt-dlp backend): best-maintained local transcript MCP; auto-paginates transcripts >50k chars (fits 58-min episodes).
claims.source: R3 / github.com/jkawamoto/mcp-youtube-transcript
claims.verifier_evidence: v0.5.7 Jan 2026, deps bumped Feb 2026, CI green; runs on the session's own IP (Mac only)

claims.claim: claude-self-reflect install-safety: PASSES the bar - default fully local (SQLite + FastEmbed in one binary), no keys, no Docker; only AI-Narratives feature is opt-in cloud; v8.2.0 notes show active security fixes.
claims.source: R3 / github.com/ramakay/claude-self-reflect

claims.claim: supabase/agent-skills (FIRST-PARTY): two instruction-only skills (supabase, postgres-best-practices); pure SKILL.md, no code execution; the Supabase MCP itself recommends it.
claims.source: R3 / github.com/supabase/agent-skills
claims.verifier_evidence: v0.1.5 2026-05-27, 2.2k stars; npx skills add supabase/agent-skills; [video jNpH_hOFvg4] Skills confirmed as markdown instruction files, context-efficient. Presenter from Replit confirms skill support added to Replit agent using same skills.sh framework, consistent with Anthropic's skills spec.
claims.gsr_application: INSTALL candidate (near-zero risk): accuracy boost on every migration/RLS/edge-function task

claims.claim: vercel-labs/next-skills (FIRST-PARTY): instruction-only skills explicitly covering Next.js 16 (cacheComponents, use cache, cacheLife/cacheTag/updateTag) - the exact training-data gap the repo warns about.
claims.source: R3 / github.com/vercel-labs/next-skills

claims.gsr_application: INSTALL candidate (near-zero risk): npx skills add vercel-labs/next-skills --skill next-best-practices

claims.claim: commit-commands plugin (official Anthropic marketplace): one-command commit/push/PR flows; first-party.
claims.source: R3 / github.com/anthropics/claude-plugins-official
claims.verifier_evidence: /plugin install commit-commands@claude-plugins-official; PR-based flow matches repo branch rules
claims.gsr_application: INSTALL candidate: cuts the most frequent manual chore; sibling pr-review-toolkit SKIPped (duplicates built-in review skills)

claims.claim: georgeantonopoulos/Basecamp-MCP-Server: 75 tools over Basecamp API incl. writes; BUT stores OAuth tokens in plaintext oauth_tokens.json by default (violates the 1Password/env-only bar) unless redirected via BASECAMP_MCP_TOKEN_FILE.
claims.source: R3 / github.com/georgeantonopoulos/Basecamp-MCP-Server
claims.verifier_evidence: 91 commits, no tagged releases, 89 stars; third-party write access needs a code skim
claims.gsr_application: WATCH: highest input-reduction potential but needs a 30-min vetting pass (token storage + write-path review) before proposing to Daniel; Lane 5 builds its own refresh loop regardless (CL-021)

claims.claim: alxpark/propresenter-mcp: full ProPresenter REST API as MCP (231 endpoints incl. write/trigger ops); local-network to port 50000.
claims.source: R3 / github.com/alxpark/propresenter-mcp

claims.claim: Whisper transcription skills exist (jftuga/transcript-critic, madappgang transcription skill) but Daniel's Mac already runs a local whisper pipeline at ~/Productions.

claims.claim: PR #50 ships the rename migration + ~20 call-site updates + display_duration/last_line on production_graphics; PR #52 (stacked) DEFERS the Title->Intro Graphic CHECK+data standardization to a follow-up migration, sets the validator to soft amber >65 (66-70 allowed, never blocks) with extraction prompt 60-65/70-ceiling, and persists Var 1/Var 2 into lower_thirds_variations slots 2/3 (slot 1 = primary) across all three write paths.
claims.source: open-PR audit 2026-06-11 (PR #50 + #52 bodies)

claims.claim: WhisperKit v1.0.0 (May 2026, repo renamed argmax-oss-swift) bundles SpeakerKit for speaker labels; its 2.2% WER is a vendor benchmark.
claims.source: docs/_handoff/VERIFIED-FACTS.md (verified 2026-06-04)

claims.claim: Kilauea 'Episode 48' is USGS's numbered lava-fountaining episode count in the Halemaumau eruption (began Dec 23 2024), NOT a TV episode number; episode 48 ran June 1 2026 (~650 ft fountains) and 'set a new record number of fountaining episodes in any one Kilauea eruption', passing Puu Oo 1983-86's 47. Daniel's read was correct.
claims.source: USGS HVO: usgs.gov/observatories/hvo/news/photo-video-chronology-episode-48-kilauea-summit-a-new-record (fetched 2026-06-11)
claims.verifier_evidence: USGS verbatim quote fetched from the page; corroborated by watchers.news 2026-06-02. Caveat: episode 49 was forecast ~Jun 12-15, so RE-CHECK the live count at usgs.gov/volcanoes/kilauea/volcano-updates on record day and swap the number. Scope guard: say 'any Kilauea eruption', never 'biggest eruption ever'. NOTE: no Austin/Kilauea episode row exists in the DB yet (verified by SELECT), so the wording is parked here + canon until Lane 3 creates the row; on-air-safe wording recorded in canon s14 resolution.
claims.gsr_application: Plan 4.4 resolved: on-air wording locked, count qualified by date; Dr. Steve Austin exists in guests but is linked to no episode yet

claims.claim: creationsuperstore.com is WordPress+WooCommerce with the entire /wp-json/ tree (incl. WooCommerce Store API) host-blocked 403; the workable lookup is HTML search (?s=<q>&post_type=product, browser UA, 48/page) with a deterministic empty-result signal ('No products were found matching your selection'), plus 3 Yoast product sitemaps (~2,113 products) for a cached slug index.
claims.source: live probes 2026-06-11 (sitemap.xml, /products.json 404, /wp-json 403, three search queries incl. an empty-contract test)
claims.verifier_evidence: Example: 'steve austin' returned 12 real products incl. Footprints in the Ash, the exact resource named in the June Topic 09 interview doc; nonsense query returned the no-products signal
claims.gsr_application: Spec committed (2026-06-11-superstore-lookup-spec.md); build lands in plan 7.4 with the never-fabricate empty contract

claims.claim: Agent teams: teammate sessions inherit ALL permissions from the main orchestrator session — bypass mode, allowed bash commands, MCP servers, and skills all carry over automatically. New teammates start with no conversation history but can read all project files.
claims.source: video vDVSGVpB2vc How to Build Claude Agent Teams Better Than 99% of People
claims.verifier_evidence: Consistent with Claude Code agent teams docs: teammates inherit the spawning session's tool permissions and settings.
claims.gsr_application: When spinning up a GSR research or script-prep agent team, permission allowlists set on the main session propagate to all subagents without re-configuration.

claims.claim: Agent teams in tmux: running Claude Code in a tmux terminal splits each teammate into its own visible pane (color-coded: blue, green, yellow per agent). You can directly message any individual teammate pane without going through the main orchestrator session.
claims.source: video vDVSGVpB2vc How to Build Claude Agent Teams Better Than 99% of People
claims.gsr_application: For multi-session GSR research runs, tmux gives a live split-screen view of each agent's progress and allows direct mid-run steering of any single agent.

claims.claim: Agent teams: plan approval mode can be configured so each teammate must have its plan approved by the main orchestrator before executing. Optionally, the human can be set as the approver instead.
claims.source: video vDVSGVpB2vc How to Build Claude Agent Teams Better Than 99% of People
claims.gsr_application: GSR research teams could gate execution behind a human-approval step to prevent runaway research or costly API calls before Daniel confirms the plan.

claims.claim: Agent teams: to trigger team creation, the prompt must explicitly include a phrase like 'create an agent team to build this out' — Claude Code will not autonomously spin up a team without this instruction. The minimum syntax is natural language specifying team creation; more specific agent role definitions (front-end agent, back-end agent, devops agent) are optional but improve results.
claims.source: video HlqAe1LL7U8 How to Build Claude Code for Free (API Costs Compared)
claims.gsr_application: GSR multi-agent research or script-prep prompts must include explicit 'create an agent team' language or agents will not be spawned.

claims.claim: Supabase background job queue pattern: create a table (e.g. background_jobs) with status (pending/processing/completed/failed) and payload columns. API route inserts a row and returns immediately. A Supabase Edge Function fetches one pending row, sets it to processing, runs the heavy logic (AI calls, PDF generation, etc.), then updates to completed/failed. pg_cron triggers the edge function every minute to poll for pending jobs.
claims.source: video oUOZlBr-smQ Claude + n8n: Make Claude Do Work For You Automatically
claims.verifier_evidence: Supabase docs confirm pg_cron can invoke edge functions on a schedule; the table-as-queue + edge function worker is a documented Supabase production pattern.
claims.gsr_application: GSR's RC import, lower-thirds generation, and AI script extraction are all slow operations that would benefit from this queue pattern to avoid Vercel timeout limits and keep the dashboard UI responsive.

claims.claim: Agent View critical bug: background agents continue running after the terminal application is fully quit. There is no quit warning, no menu bar indicator, and no system notification when agents remain alive. The only way to discover a zombie agent is to open a new terminal and run 'claude agents'. This can silently consume API credits or drain a Max plan rate limit for hours undetected.
claims.source: video msiaY6wec90 Claude Code Agent View Review | Skip This Until Anthropic Fixes It
claims.gsr_application: Daniel must run 'claude agents' to check for zombie sessions after closing any terminal with active background agents, especially /goal loops, to avoid invisible credit drain.

claims.claim: Agent View: the '/bg' slash command drops the current foreground Claude Code session into the background agent dashboard without interrupting it.
claims.source: video msiaY6wec90 Claude Code Agent View Review | Skip This Until Anthropic Fixes It
claims.gsr_application: Daniel can use /bg to move a long-running GSR research or RC import session to the background dashboard while starting a new foreground session.

claims.claim: Agent View: parallel background sessions all share the same working directory by default — no folder isolation. Agents writing files can overwrite each other's work. The reviewer recommends each agent be given its own subdirectory as a workaround until Anthropic adds automatic isolation.
claims.source: video msiaY6wec90 Claude Code Agent View Review | Skip This Until Anthropic Fixes It
claims.gsr_application: Any GSR parallel agent run (e.g. multiple episode research sessions) must be launched from different working directories or use explicit file-naming conventions to prevent agents stomping each other's outputs.

claims.claim: Custom sub-agents can be stored in three scopes: (1) ./.claude/agents/ for project-level shared with the team, (2) ~/.claude/agents/ for personal use across all projects, (3) via plugin system for distribution. The CLI flag --agent is session-only and saves nothing to disk. Agents can be invoked with @agentname syntax to force-call a specific agent.
claims.source: video MbKUeufUZIY Claude Code Sub-Agents: Step-by-Step Beginner Tutorial
claims.gsr_application: GSR's gsr-architect agent should live in .claude/agents/ so it is version-controlled and available to all team Claude sessions without manual setup.

claims.claim: The Claude Code task system exposes four tools to agents: TaskCreate, TaskGet, TaskList, TaskUpdate. Both primary agents and sub-agents can use these tools to coordinate work. Sub-agents ping the primary agent on completion through task updates, eliminating the need for bash sleep/polling loops. The task system activates by default when a prompt is large enough and the model is Opus.
claims.source: video 4_2j5wgt_ds Claude Code Task System: ANTI-HYPE Agentic Coding (Advanced)
claims.gsr_application: The GSR multi-stage pipeline (research -> scoring -> RC import -> lower-thirds) maps naturally to a task list where each stage blocks the next, with agents pinging completion rather than requiring manual session handoffs.

claims.claim: Claude Code agent teams have no native persistence of agent work logs after a session ends. Gastown (open-source multi-agent tool using gt rig, gt crew, gt mayor, gt dashboard commands) offers more mature persistence and interrogation of prior worker context across session restarts, at the cost of a steeper learning curve.
claims.source: video vQkJaM2NZPc Claude code teams vs Gastown: Multi-Agent orchestration compared
claims.gsr_application: For long-running GSR automation tasks that span multiple days, Gastown's persistence model may be worth evaluating vs native agent teams once teams stabilize.

claims.claim: Oh-My-ClaudeCode (OMC) is a Claude Code plugin with 31K stars installable from the plugin marketplace. It provides a /team <n> <executor_type> <description> skill that spawns N agents without requiring manual agent configuration. Team orchestration requires tmux. It supports Claude, Codex, and Gemini as agent providers via an OpenClaw gateway for forwarding session events.
claims.source: video bOrKWA18PZ4 Oh-My-ClaudeCode: Visualize Multi-agent Orchestration with One Prompt
claims.gsr_application: OMC could simplify spinning up ad-hoc GSR research agent teams without requiring full agent .md file authoring for one-off tasks.

claims.claim: The orchestrator-headless pattern for long-running autonomous loops: package an orchestrator as a Claude skill that calls claude -p <prompt> for each iteration, keeping the orchestrator's context window clean. Each claude -p invocation gets a fresh context (not reporting back to orchestrator), unlike sub-agents which report results back and inflate the orchestrator's context.
claims.source: video XzlSn1M6WKw Stop Using Claude's /goal Feature | Here's What Works
claims.gsr_application: For GSR's every-5-hour Studio v4 scheduled task, this pattern could reduce context buildup across long-running production pipeline loops.

claims.claim: OpenTelemetry (OTEL) support in Claude Code is now available to Max and Pro plan subscribers, not just enterprise. The built-in Claude Code GUI dashboard is fed from OTEL data, and the full OTEL spec can be used to build custom observability dashboards reading both JSONL logs and OTEL streams.
claims.source: video dITtLiC9FzM Claude Code + OpenTelemetry = Claude Command Center
claims.gsr_application: Daniel can instrument gsr-automation-v2 session activity via OTEL on his Max plan without needing enterprise access, enabling real token/latency visibility across parallel sessions.

claims.claim: CC Lens (npx cclens, github arindam200/cclens) is a local-only open-source dashboard that reads ~/.claude JSONL files and provides project/session/model/activity breakdowns, per-session token costs, tool call frequency by type (bash, read, MCP), memory contents viewer, and session export. No telemetry leaves the machine.
claims.source: video F01R99FeB5U I built an Open Source Analytics Dashboard for Claude Code (100% Free)
claims.verifier_evidence: https://github.com/arindam200/cclens
claims.gsr_application: Alternative to ccusage for Daniel to audit which sessions are expensive and which MCP tools are called most; useful for diagnosing RC or Supabase MCP latency issues.

claims.claim: Hooks that invoke an LLM add significant latency to every matched tool call. A creator who built many LLM-based hooks removed most of them because they made the workflow frustratingly slow. Best practice: keep hooks as deterministic code; only call an LLM inside a hook for genuinely subjective checks, and be selective.
claims.source: video G9OBGn0h6LE Claude Code Hooks: Get Notified When Tasks Finish
claims.gsr_application: GSR pipeline hooks (e.g., lower-thirds validation, RC write guards) should stay deterministic shell scripts; calling Claude inside a PreToolUse hook will slow every single tool call in a session.

claims.claim: Hook trigger matching in settings.json can only match on tool type (Write, Edit, Bash), not on specific commands or file patterns. To filter further (e.g., only fire on mkdir, only on .md files), the hook script itself must inspect the incoming command/path and exit early if not relevant.
claims.source: video G9OBGn0h6LE Claude Code Hooks: Get Notified When Tasks Finish
claims.gsr_application: When writing GSR pipeline hooks (e.g., guard against RC writes without dry-run), the settings.json entry must match Bash broadly; the script body must check the actual command pattern before acting.

claims.claim: Claude Agent SDK query() options include permissionMode: 'bypassPermissions' and dangerouslySkipPermissions: true to run fully unattended without approval prompts. The response is an async iterable of message objects; the final message has type 'result'. allowedTools array controls which built-in tools the agent can use.
claims.source: video ChaQ_tZDBFg You Can Build The Craziest Things with Claude's Agent SDK
claims.gsr_application: For unattended GSR automation (nightly RC sync, scheduled lower-thirds generation), set bypassPermissions so the agent doesn't stall waiting for human approval.

claims.claim: Claude Agent SDK structured outputs were recently released (as of the workshop date). The presenter said 'we just released this — Google agent SDK structured outputs.' This enables typed/schema-constrained responses from agent runs, useful for workflow-style agents that need deterministic output shapes.
claims.source: video TqC1qOfiVcQ Claude Agent SDK [Full Workshop] - Thariq Shihipar, Anthropic
claims.gsr_application: GSR pipeline agents (extract lower thirds, score guests) can use structured outputs to guarantee JSON schema conformance without post-processing heuristics.

claims.claim: Anthropic's internal recommendation: every agent needs a container or local file system because the Agent SDK is built on Claude Code and requires bash + file system to operate. Sub-agents protect the main agent's context window by doing work and returning only the summary result. The Agent SDK handles compaction, tool-use errors, and sub-agent orchestration so builders focus on domain logic.
claims.source: video TqC1qOfiVcQ Claude Agent SDK [Full Workshop] - Thariq Shihipar, Anthropic
claims.gsr_application: When Daniel builds multi-stage GSR pipeline agents (research -> scoring -> script), spin up sub-agents per stage rather than one long context; each sub-agent returns its summary to the orchestrator.

claims.claim: MCP servers load all tool descriptions into the context window upfront, making them context-heavy. Skills (markdown files) are context-efficient: only the skill description loads until the skill is actually invoked, at which point the full markdown is read. For sub-agents with limited context, prefer assigning one MCP server per sub-agent rather than loading all MCPs into every agent.
claims.source: video jNpH_hOFvg4 Build & deploy agents with the Claude Agent SDK
claims.gsr_application: In GSR's multi-agent pipeline, give the RC sub-agent only the RC MCP, and the Supabase sub-agent only the Supabase MCP, to avoid context pollution that degrades accuracy.

claims.claim: Claude Agent SDK can be invoked from a FastAPI web server with skills loaded from a local .claude/skills/ directory. Setting cwd in the query options determines which project's .claude folder (and thus which skills) the agent reads. This enables exposing Claude Code skills as HTTP endpoints on any server environment including cloud-deployed containers.
claims.source: video DU6ozVjWep8 Claude Code Skills Using Claude Agent SDK
claims.gsr_application: GSR's Next.js API routes already call Claude server-side; this confirms that Agent SDK with skill loading from the project's .claude/skills/ folder is a valid pattern for the gsr-automation-v2 codebase.

claims.claim: Claude Agent SDK overhead is approximately 28,000 input tokens per call regardless of prompt size. Each agent run costs roughly 30-60 cents at API rates. Three env vars redirect the SDK to any OpenAI-compatible provider: ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL (no /v1 suffix for Anthropic-compatible endpoint), ANTHROPIC_API_KEY='' (blank to override stored login), ANTHROPIC_DEFAULT_MODEL (model name). The SDK auto-appends a '1m' context-size suffix to model names.
claims.source: video Egt3DhQThA8 Claude Agent SDK Tutorial: Build an AI Coding Agent with Gemma 4
claims.gsr_application: Baseline cost awareness for GSR pipeline: each automated Agent SDK invocation (lower-thirds generation, RC sync) costs ~28k tokens overhead before any prompt content, relevant when estimating Max plan usage across 5-10 parallel sessions.

claims.claim: The official skill-creator skill (available via /plugins in Claude Code, anthropic namespace) can evaluate, benchmark, and trigger-tune existing skills. It runs evals comparing skill vs no-skill outputs, produces pass rate / token / time benchmarks, and rewrites skill descriptions to improve trigger accuracy. Two skill types: capability uplift (teaching new behaviors, may fade as models improve) vs encoded preference (workflows specific to the user, durable).
claims.source: video RAZVk5NPNtE Claude Code Skills Just Got Even Better
claims.gsr_application: Daniel can install skill-creator and run evals on GSR editorial skills (lower thirds format, hook framework) to catch regressions when Anthropic updates the underlying model, and improve trigger accuracy so skills fire on natural language prompts.

claims.claim: Skill YAML frontmatter supports a disable_model_invocation field (default false). When false, Claude Code can invoke the skill autonomously based on natural language matching the skill description. Setting it true requires explicit slash command invocation. The description field is the only part loaded into context for skill discovery; the full skill body only loads on invocation.
claims.source: video RAZVk5NPNtE Claude Code Skills Just Got Even Better
claims.gsr_application: For GSR skills that should only run on explicit request (e.g., RC import), set disable_model_invocation: true to prevent accidental triggering during unrelated sessions.

claims.claim: The `ultrathink` keyword in a Claude Code prompt allocates the maximum thinking budget of approximately 32,000 tokens before Claude responds. Use it for architecture decisions, complex debugging, or when normal prompting is not producing good results after multiple tries — not for simple fixes.
claims.source: video jqoFP9QapXI 32 Tricks to Level Up Claude Code in 16 Mins
claims.gsr_application: Use ultrathink when GSR pipeline decisions affect the whole system (schema migrations, RC API integration architecture, multi-session orchestration design).

claims.claim: Claude Code supports a `--worktree <feature-name>` CLI flag that creates an isolated git branch workspace, allowing multiple Claude sessions to work on the same project simultaneously without overwriting each other. Branches merge back like normal git branches.
claims.source: video jqoFP9QapXI 32 Tricks to Level Up Claude Code in 16 Mins
claims.gsr_application: Run parallel Claude sessions for GSR dashboard features (e.g., lower-thirds UI and RC import logic) without branch collisions.

claims.claim: `/compact` accepts inline scope instructions, e.g. `/compact but keep all API integration decisions and database schema` — Claude compresses the conversation but preserves the specified content. Combined with `/context` which shows token consumption breakdown by category (system prompt, file contents, MCP servers) as percentages.
claims.source: video jqoFP9QapXI 32 Tricks to Level Up Claude Code in 16 Mins
claims.gsr_application: When GSR sessions bloat from RC API calls and schema context, use /context to diagnose then /compact with specific retention instructions to stay productive.

claims.claim: The agent teams video (oC3F2SFaF9w) demonstrates that using tmux alongside agent teams allows viewing and interacting with each individual agent in its own pane simultaneously, which is not possible in the default single-conversation view. The presenter calls this 'T-Max' (tmux). A single agent teams run for social media content generation cost approximately $7-8 USD, consuming about 50% of a single Pro session limit.
claims.source: video oC3F2SFaF9w Claude Code's Agent Teams Are Insane (Build Your AI Workforce)
claims.gsr_application: Multi-session GSR orchestration (research + booking + script prep in parallel) could use tmux to monitor each specialized agent independently; budget $7-8 per complex multi-step task.

claims.claim: trigger.dev is a hosted TypeScript workflow runtime with scheduled runs, automatic retries, queuing, and orchestration. A trigger.dev MCP server exists that Claude Code can use to control trigger.dev directly from within a session. Production deployment is via GitHub repo sync: every push to the tracked branch auto-deploys to the trigger.dev production environment. Environment variables must be added separately in trigger.dev (not from .env file) for both development and production environments.
claims.source: video UGIZnh6HNLc The EASIEST Way to Host Your Claude Code Agents
claims.gsr_application: GSR background automations (YouTube transcript checks, RC poller, guest outreach scheduler) can be deployed to trigger.dev for always-on cloud execution without requiring Daniel's laptop to stay open.

claims.claim: Claude Code had a caching bug in March 2026 that inflated token costs by 10-20x, causing Max Plan users to drain their entire session in under 2 hours. Anthropic acknowledged the bug. As of the video (April 2026), it appeared to be resolved. The terminal CLI is technically the most feature-complete mode; the VS Code extension covers almost everything but a few advanced features remain CLI-only.
claims.source: video 99VHENEKA9o 100 Hours Testing Claude Code vs Antigravity (honest results)
claims.gsr_application: If session costs spike unexpectedly, check for a recurrence of the caching bug. For accessing all features, prefer the terminal CLI over VS Code extension for production GSR pipeline work.

claims.claim: Cursor Automations (cloud-based, event-driven coding agents) require an open Cursor IDE to run locally; they run in cloud sandboxes when using their hosted product. In contrast, Claude Code's Cogram/desktop scheduled tasks require the desktop app to stay open — if the computer is off, those scheduled tasks do not run. This is the gap that trigger.dev or similar hosting fills.
claims.source: video NDnv16PY2XQ Cursor Automations Clearly Explained (worth learning?)
claims.verifier_evidence: Claude Code desktop scheduled tasks are documented as running locally; cloud Routines are the Anthropic-managed alternative per CL-027.
claims.gsr_application: Confirms that GSR scheduled tasks using Claude desktop app will fail when Daniel's machine is off; use trigger.dev or Routines (cloud) for always-on automations.

claims.claim: Anthropic's official policy (stated in Agent SDK docs): unless previously approved, Anthropic does not allow third-party developers to offer Claude.ai login or pass-through of plan rate limits for their products, including agents built on the Agent SDK. Using a Claude subscription inside third-party harnesses like OpenClaw is not permitted without Anthropic approval.
claims.source: video RLjaUES9P8A 100 Hours Testing Claude Code vs ChatGPT Codex (honest results)
claims.gsr_application: Confirms that GSR tooling must use Anthropic's own interfaces (Claude Code, API key) rather than any third-party harness that tunnels the Claude subscription -- relevant if evaluating workflow runners.

claims.claim: Remote Claude Code routines clone the linked GitHub repository into a cloud environment at run time, execute the prompt, then destroy that environment. Any file changes the routine writes (memory files, logs, trade journals) must be committed and pushed back to the repo during the run, or the next routine invocation will not see them. The routine settings include a permission toggle called 'allow unrestricted branch pushes' that must be enabled for Claude to push to any branch.
claims.source: video 6MC1XqZSltw I Turned Claude Into a 24/7 Trader
claims.gsr_application: GSR routines that update episode state, lower-thirds drafts, or research notes must include an explicit push-back step in the routine prompt, and the 'allow unrestricted branch pushes' permission must be on, or state will be lost between runs.

claims.claim: API keys for remote Claude Code routines must be stored as named environment variables in the cloud environment configuration (set in the Claude desktop app under the environment's settings), not in a .env file inside the repo. The environment variable names in the routine prompt must match exactly letter-for-letter the names configured in the environment, or the routine cannot find them.
claims.source: video 6MC1XqZSltw I Turned Claude Into a 24/7 Trader
claims.gsr_application: GSR's Supabase, Rundown Creator, and Anthropic API keys for remote routines must be entered in the cloud environment settings panel, not in any .env committed to the GitHub repo, and the routine prompt must reference them by exact variable name.

claims.claim: Claude Design is a new Anthropic Labs product (research preview as of June 2026) for building prototypes, slide decks, and one-pagers via natural language. It is powered by Opus 4.7. It supports a design system feature where brand assets and guidelines are ingested once and applied to all subsequent projects. Finished designs can be handed off to Claude Code via a generated copy command that fetches the design file as a zip and implements it in a local project. Exports include Canva, PDF, PowerPoint, and HTML.
claims.source: video gAoZ95kqG7w Claude Design Just Became Unstoppable
claims.gsr_application: GSR could use Claude Design to prototype lower-thirds layouts, slide templates for internal presentations, or show graphics concepts before handing them off to Claude Code for implementation, keeping everything in the Anthropic ecosystem.

claims.claim: The author's practical heuristic for choosing between n8n and Claude Code: if a use case can be built in n8n in 10 minutes or less, build it there because publishing, error workflows, execution logs, and triggers are already configured. Use Claude Code for complex iterative builds or tasks that need deep reasoning. The two tools are complementary, not substitutional, and existing n8n automations do not need to be migrated.
claims.source: video wb4uDdsFh6o Is n8n Dead?
claims.gsr_application: Reinforces CL-024 with an actionable decision rule: GSR's simple webhook-triggered notifications or data-sync automations belong in n8n; the pipeline stages requiring multi-step reasoning or file manipulation belong in Claude Code.
